name: LaunchPad India
description: India-focused startup and product launch directory for discovering and showcasing Indian-made and India-serving products to a local founder, maker and early-adopter audience.

auth.notes: Email/password account to submit and manage listings. Submissions are reviewed before going live; a paid tier expedites the queue and featured placement.

best_practices: LaunchPad India's audience is Indian founders, makers and early adopters — call out the India angle (built in India, India-first pricing, or solving an India-specific problem) where it's genuine.
Keep the tagline plain-English and jargon-free; many readers skim on mobile, so front-load what it does.
If you price in INR or offer India-friendly payments (UPI, Razorpay), mention it — it builds trust with this crowd.
Pick the single most accurate category rather than spreading across many; the directory is browsed by category.
Use a clean square logo that reads well at small sizes in the listing grid.
Add your founder/maker name and socials — the community engages directly with people behind the product.
List concrete benefits over a feature dump; describe who it's for and the outcome they get.

questions.label: Tagline / one-liner
questions.help: One plain-English sentence on what it does and for whom.

questions.label: Description
questions.help: What the product is, the problem it solves and who it's for; mention the India angle if relevant.

questions.label: Pricing
questions.help: Free, freemium or paid; mention INR / UPI support if applicable.
